name: Windows utilities · System tray tools (multi_scripts_lib/work)
description: System tray utilities under multi_scripts_lib/work/system tray—long-lived Windows helpers where least privilege, signing, and graceful degradation matter.
longDescription: Lightweight utilities under `multi_scripts_lib/work/system tray` for background tasks reachable from the Windows system tray without a full application window. Tray-resident code is treated as high visibility on the endpoint: failures should be quiet but observable, and privileges should stay minimal unless a feature explicitly requires elevation.
features: Targets system tray interaction patterns without unnecessary foreground windows
Defensive handling when tray APIs or session state are unavailable
highlights: Always-available helpers from the system tray
challenges: Tray apps must fail gracefully when permissions or session state changes.
solutions: Document startup requirements and handle missing tray APIs defensively in code.
security: Tray apps are high-visibility targets—sign binaries and minimise privileges
